Home → Demo Hub → Enterprise Pilot Room

Enterprise evaluation surface

Enterprise Pilot Room

A pilot-ready review room for evaluating The Agent Command Center with clear scope, roles, success criteria, risks, safety boundaries, and go/no-go decisions. This room prepares an enterprise pilot review. It does not grant runtime permission.

Pilot Review Mode Stakeholder Checklists Audit Trails No Execution

Safety boundary

Pilot approval is not command execution. Limited pilot approval does not activate the full fleet. Any future runtime authorization must be explicit, scoped, logged, and separately approved. No page in this pilot room runs shell commands, deploys, rollbacks, alerts, or arbitrary actions.

No command execution Scoped approval only.
Pilot statusunknown
Pilot scopeunknown
Live runtime agents0
Permission grantedfalse

Recommended Next Step

Review stakeholder package and log decision.

What this pilot is

  • A controlled enterprise review.
  • A verification of safety boundaries.
  • An audit of system readiness.

What this pilot is not

  • Not command execution.
  • Not a grant of runtime permission.
  • Not full fleet activation.

Pilot scope

Total registered agents: 47,979
Total departments: 1,777
Total units: 5,331
Total families: 175
Full fleet cap: 47,979

© 2026 Devin O’Rourke. All rights reserved.

Agent Command Center, the demo pages, registry presentation, dashboard copy, visual layout, simulator materials, validator descriptions, and stakeholder presentation materials are protected project materials. No license, sale, transfer, or permission to reuse is granted by viewing this demo.

This site is a public stakeholder review surface. Some pages are static demonstrations, and some pages may be controlled backend-wired status, audit, readiness, approval, or export surfaces. No page enables raw activate-all, arbitrary shell execution, arbitrary SQL, command execution, deployment actions, rollback execution, alert sending, or unrestricted automation.

Third-party names, platforms, and services referenced for context remain the property of their respective owners.
